meta.note: Second pass: per-country censorship sweep for UG/TZ/NG/MA/ZW (Tier 1 high-confidence + Tier 2 medium). Each candidate was deduped against the DB and re-sourced to a primary/reputable URL. Performance-only theatre bans and low-confidence holds (I Am Malala-TZ, Stolen Lives, NG challenges) were excluded by decision. Two entries use existing_book_id to ADD a country-ban to a book already in the DB (Fanon, ABC of Equality). banned-books.org self-citations were rejected as circular.

entries.title: Le Passé simple
entries.description_ban: Chraïbi's debut novel attacking patriarchal Moroccan and Islamic tradition was read as heretical and banned in Morocco, sparking a decades-long literary controversy ('l'affaire Chraïbi').
entries.inclusion_rationale: Canonical case of Moroccan religious censorship; documented by the New York Review of Books.
entries.source_name: The New York Review of Books

entries.title: Sahih Al-Bukhari: The End of a Legend
entries.description_ban: A Marrakech court banned the book from sale in 2018; the governor deemed it a threat to citizens' 'spiritual security' for questioning the Hadith canon. The author received death threats.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Court-ordered sale ban over religious content; documented by Raseef22.

entries.title: The Commander of the Faithful: The Moroccan Political Elite
entries.description_ban: The Arabic translation has long been banned in Morocco; its analysis of Hassan II's control of the political elite alarmed authorities. Ban year approximate (Arabic edition).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Long-standing political ban of the Arabic edition; listed by The New Arab.

entries.title: Les Officiers de Sa Majesté
entries.description_ban: Ex-army officer's exposé of corruption and arms-deal kickbacks in the Moroccan army's highest ranks; banned in Morocco.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Political ban of a military exposé; listed by The New Arab.

entries.title: Mohammed VI: le grand malentendu
entries.description_ban: Moroccan journalist Ali Amar's critical account of the first decade of Mohammed VI's reign, with details of private royal meetings; banned in Morocco.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Political ban of a monarchy-critical book; reported by The New Arab and Africa Intelligence.

entries.title: Mohammed VI: Derrière les masques
entries.description_ban: Moroccan journalist Omar Brouksy's critical portrait of the king; listed among Morocco's banned monarchy books.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Political ban of a monarchy-critical book; listed by The New Arab (medium confidence).

entries.title: Mémoires d'un soldat marocain
entries.description_ban: Former army officer's memoir alleging the army's involvement in the drug trade; banned in Morocco.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Political ban of a military memoir; listed by The New Arab (medium confidence).

entries.title: Summary of the General History of Africa
entries.description_ban: Withdrawn at the 2018 Casablanca International Book Fair (SIEL) for printing a map of Morocco without Western Sahara; one of 25 titles pulled that year.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Book-fair withdrawal over territorial-integrity grounds; reported by Morocco World News (medium confidence).

entries.title: Coming of the Dry Season
entries.description_ban: The Rhodesian Censorship Board banned this short-story collection because the story 'The Accident' was read as a subtle attack on the colonial political order.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Rhodesia-era censorship-board ban of a landmark Black Zimbabwean work; covered in Zimbabwean literary press.

entries.title: Mindblast
entries.description_ban: On the day of publication Marechera was arrested and held six days under the Emergency Powers Act over content deemed insulting to the post-independence revolution.
entries.inclusion_rationale: State suppression of a published work via author detention; documented in UNISA Press scholarship (Vhutuza 2017, citing Veit-Wild).

entries.title: On Trial for My Country
entries.description_ban: Historical novel on the Rhodes–Lobengula clash, banned by the Rhodesian government for its anti-colonial themes.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Rhodesia-era political ban of an anti-colonial novel (medium confidence).

entries.title: The Gospel According to the Ghetto
entries.description_ban: Black-liberation-theology rewriting of scripture by the future first President of Zimbabwe; banned in Rhodesia.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Rhodesia-era religious/political ban (medium confidence).

entries.title: Origins of Rhodesia
entries.description_ban: Listed on Rhodesia's 1959–79 prohibited-publications register (compiled from Julie Frederikse's 'None but Ourselves'). Ban year set to publication year; exact gazette year within the UDI period unknown.
entries.inclusion_rationale: On the Rhodesia 1959–79 banned-imports list (single-source, Frederikse; medium confidence).

entries.title: No Easy Walk to Freedom
entries.description_ban: Listed on Rhodesia's 1959–79 prohibited-publications register (Frederikse, 'None but Ourselves'). Ban year set to publication year; exact gazette year within the UDI period unknown.
entries.inclusion_rationale: On the Rhodesia 1959–79 banned-imports list (single-source, Frederikse; medium confidence).

entries.title: How Europe Underdeveloped Africa
entries.description_ban: Listed on Rhodesia's 1959–79 prohibited-publications register (Frederikse, 'None but Ourselves'). Ban year set to publication year; exact gazette year within the UDI period unknown.
entries.inclusion_rationale: On the Rhodesia 1959–79 banned-imports list (single-source, Frederikse; medium confidence).

entries.title: The Female Eunuch
entries.description_ban: Listed on Rhodesia's 1959–79 prohibited-publications register (Frederikse, 'None but Ourselves'), on the sex/morals grounds typical of the period. Ban year set to publication year; exact gazette year unknown.
entries.inclusion_rationale: On the Rhodesia 1959–79 banned-imports list (single-source, Frederikse; medium confidence).

entries.title: Phantom Crescent
entries.description_ban: An Upper Sharia Court in Kaduna State issued an injunction (Oct 2007) barring anyone from selling or circulating Sani's satirical work on Sharia-law abuses; the manuscript ban stood even after the court later declined jurisdiction.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Sharia-court injunction banning sale/circulation; reported by AllAfrica, BBC and FT.

entries.title: The People: Facts That You Must Know
entries.description_ban: Dynamic Party leader Chike Obi was prosecuted for sedition over this pamphlet calling the government 'enemies of the people'; the conviction was upheld by the Federal Supreme Court (DPP v Chike Obi, 1961), a landmark Nigerian sedition case.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Landmark sedition prosecution; primary court report on NigeriaLII.

entries.title: How Jim Nwobodo Rules Anambra State
entries.description_ban: Nwankwo was tried and jailed 12 months for sedition over this book criticising the Anambra governor; the conviction was overturned on appeal (Nwankwo v State (1985) 6 NCLR 228), a leading free-expression precedent.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Sedition prosecution and landmark appeal; documented in Nigerian case law.

entries.title: The Tears of a Bride
entries.description_ban: Withdrawn from school exam reading lists after a PTA challenge over 'indecent' content (rape, defilement); a National Assembly resolution (Oct 2017) directed the education ministry to withdraw it. Remained available outside schools.
entries.inclusion_rationale: School-list withdrawal via National Assembly resolution; documented in Ifeduba's academic study.

entries.title: The Precious Child
entries.description_ban: Recommended for School Certificate exams, then withdrawn from school lists after a PTA challenge for 'corrupting young minds', under the Oct 2017 National Assembly resolution.
entries.inclusion_rationale: School-list withdrawal via National Assembly resolution; documented in Ifeduba's academic study.

entries.title: In Dependence
entries.description_ban: JAMB-recommended text challenged for 'indecent and corrupting' content and withdrawn from school lists under the Oct 2017 National Assembly resolution.
entries.inclusion_rationale: School-list withdrawal via National Assembly resolution; documented in Ifeduba's academic study.

entries.title: People Power: Battle the Mighty General
entries.description_ban: Ugandan police seized 106 copies from the author's home and charged him with inciting violence over this book urging Museveni to resign; documented in PEN International's 2011 Case List.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Police seizure and prosecution over a political book; PEN International Case List 2011.

entries.title: Betrayed by My Leader: The Memoirs of John Kazoora
entries.description_ban: Memoir by a former NRM 'bush war' cadre critical of Museveni; widely reported as pulled from Ugandan bookshops. Listed in Wikipedia's 'List of books banned by governments' (Uganda).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Reported retail ban of a regime-critical memoir (medium confidence).
entries.source_name: Wikipedia (List of books banned by governments)

entries.title: Kizza Besigye and Uganda's Unfinished Revolution
entries.description_ban: The author was stopped at the Malaba border and prevented from bringing copies of his Besigye political biography into Uganda; documented by Chapter Four Uganda.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Border interdiction of a political biography; documented by Chapter Four Uganda (medium confidence).

entries.title: Is It the Fundamental Change?
entries.description_ban: Makerere student activists were arrested in April 2012 as they prepared to launch this Museveni-critical book; the launch was suppressed. Title also rendered 'Fundamental Change: Unveiling the Hidden Truth'.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Suppressed book launch and arrests; reported by Daily Monitor and Chapter Four Uganda (medium confidence).

entries.title: Black Skin, White Masks
entries.description_ban: Listed on Rhodesia's 1959–79 prohibited-publications register (Frederikse, 'None but Ourselves'). Ban year is an approximation within the UDI period.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Rhodesia/Zimbabwe ban to an existing book record; on the Frederikse banned-imports list (medium confidence).

entries.title: An ABC of Equality, Vol 1
entries.description_ban: Part of Tanzania's February 2023 Ministry of Education ban of 16 supplementary titles. The ministry mislabelled two of this picture book's text lines ('T is for Transgender', 'L is for LGBTQI') as separate banned books; they are entries inside this single title.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds the Tanzania 2023 school ban to an existing book record; the real title behind the two phantom entries in the ministerial list. Documented by Tanzanian Affairs and the US State Dept 2023 human-rights report.
